## Compute Intersubject Correlation (ISC) {.unnumbered}


### Description

Computes ISC using either the **pairwise correlation** method or the **leave-one-out** method.
The pairwise method computes a full correlation matrix and averages Fisher z-transformed values.
The leave-one-out method computes the correlation of each participant's time series with the mean of all others.



### Usage

<pre><code class='language-R'>calculate_isc(
  data_matrix,
  method = c("pairwise", "leave-one-out"),
  cor_method = c("pearson", "spearman"),
  return_matrix = FALSE
)
</code></pre>


### Arguments

<table role = "presentation">
<tr><td style = "white-space: collapse; font-family: monospace; vertical-align: top"><code id="data_matrix">data_matrix</code></td>
<td>
A numeric matrix or data.frame where rows represent time points and columns represent participants.
</td></tr>
<tr><td style = "white-space: collapse; font-family: monospace; vertical-align: top"><code id="method">method</code></td>
<td>
A string specifying the ISC computation method. Options are '&quot;pairwise&quot;' (default) or '&quot;leave-one-out&quot;'.
</td></tr>
<tr><td style = "white-space: collapse; font-family: monospace; vertical-align: top"><code id="cor_method">cor_method</code></td>
<td>
Correlation type passed to [stats::cor()]. One of '&quot;pearson&quot;' (default) or '&quot;spearman&quot;'.
</td></tr>
<tr><td style = "white-space: collapse; font-family: monospace; vertical-align: top"><code id="return_matrix">return_matrix</code></td>
<td>
Logical. If 'TRUE' and 'method = &quot;pairwise&quot;', return the full
participant-by-participant correlation matrix (self-correlations set to NA) instead of
a summary vector. Ignored for 'method = &quot;leave-one-out&quot;'.
</td></tr>
</table>


### Value

A named numeric vector of ISC values (one per participant), or a correlation matrix
if 'return_matrix = TRUE'.
